intermediate.note: Locked: both the 2020 low and 2022 high are real, independently confirmed extremes (see module docstring), not subject to promotion. Labeled 'major secular low/high' rather than a specific wave-position number ('(IV)'/'(V)') since neither anchor's PRECEDING wave structure can be independently resolved from data that starts after it -- same restraint TLT_LONG's/^IXIC's own bootstraps apply for the identical reason. The 2020 low deliberately excludes the real April 20-21, 2020 negative-price print (a contract-expiry artifact, not a representative price level) -- see negative_price_anomaly.

labels.granularity_note: real intraday value -- CL.json carries real OHLC throughout; excludes the real 2020-04-20/21 negative-price contract-expiry anomaly by construction (search window starts 04-22, see module docstring / negative_price_anomaly below)

primary_labels.wave: decline since the 2022 major secular high
primary_labels.description: Project-derived hypothesis: CL's own real price history shows a genuine, still-forming decline since the 2022-03-07 high (down roughly 48% from that peak as of this bootstrap) -- NOT labeled with a specific Elliott wave count here. Same open question as TLT_LONG's own post-2020 decline: whether this is a large A-B-C correction of the 2020-2022 advance, or the start of a new higher-degree decline, is a real question this bootstrap does not resolve -- left for human review on the deep-history panel.

alternates.label: CFTC interim staff report + EIA/Forbes/CRS coverage: the May 2020 WTI contract traded negative (-$37.62 close) on April 20, 2020, driven by near-full Cushing, OK storage ahead of the contract's April 21 expiry -- holders paid counterparties to avoid physical delivery rather than a fundamental demand collapse of that exact magnitude
alternates.caveat: Factual/regulatory confirmation of the contract-mechanics anomaly, not an Elliott Wave source -- used to justify EXCLUDING April 20-21 from this bootstrap's own low-window search, not to support any degree-naming claim.

alternates.label: CNBC (March 6-7, 2022): "West Texas Intermediate crude futures spiked to $130.50" overnight as markets priced in a possible Western ban on Russian oil imports following Russia's invasion of Ukraine
alternates.caveat: Factual price/date confirmation, exact match to CL.json's own 2022-03-07 high (130.50) -- not itself an Elliott Wave source.

alternates.label: elliottwave-forecast.com, long-term USO/crude oil cycles: the 2008 all-time high (147.27) declined in a three-swing (a-b-c) correction into the 2009 low, then a complex double-three-with-triangle bounce, with "each of the cycles" declining again in three big swings into the February 2016 lows (26.06)
alternates.caveat: An independent forecasting service's own published count, not an institutional consensus -- background only, showing this project's own 2000-2026 window sits inside an already-tracked wave count. NEITHER the 2008 high nor the 2016 low is used as this bootstrap's own anchor (2020/2022 are); included for context, not reused as a citation for the anchors themselves.

alternates.label: elliottwave-forecast.com weekly Brent Crude count: the April 2020 COVID selloff is framed as a "Super Cycle wave (II) low of $15.74" -- corroborating 2020 as a genuine major turning point independent of WTI's own contract-expiry anomaly, since Brent never traded negative
alternates.caveat: A DIFFERENT series (Brent, not WTI/CL=F) and, being 'Super Cycle', almost certainly counted against a much longer history than this project's own 26yr CL.json window supports -- an independent site's own count, not institutional consensus, and not adopted as this bootstrap's own degree name (Intermediate, per module docstring) -- same 'candidate hypothesis, out of scope' treatment ^DJI's bootstrap gives its own Norris Supercycle aside.

negative_price_anomaly.note: The May 2020 NYMEX WTI contract traded negative on these two dates (real intraday low -40.32 on 2020-04-20, close -37.63) -- a contract-mechanics artifact of the EXPIRING front-month contract (Cushing, OK storage reportedly ~83-100% full, forcing holders to pay counterparties to avoid physical delivery), not a representative price level for crude oil's own fundamental value that week. This bootstrap's low-window search explicitly starts April 22 (post-roll, June contract) to avoid anchoring on this artifact -- see module docstring. Rendered on the deep-history chart via a LINEAR (not log) price axis specifically so this real, historic dip is visible rather than silently clipped -- log(negative) is undefined.

invalidation.basis: A confirmed close below the real 2020-04-28 low (10.07) would invalidate the 'single Intermediate-degree advance 2020->2022' framing this bootstrap locks in. (Not the 2020-04-20/21 negative print itself -- see negative_price_anomaly.)

history_note: CL.json covers 2000-08-23 to 2026-07-05 (6494 rows) -- Yahoo's CL=F continuous front-month WTI future, adjustment: split_only (no back-adjustment smoothing across contract-roll gaps; roll-date discontinuities are left visible in the series, dashboard_build_spec.md §7). Own-longest-series self-anchor, independent of any other instrument's degree claim -- crude oil is its own asset class, no equity/bond/metal 'principle of commonality' stand-in applies. Includes the real, historic April 20-21, 2020 negative-price print (see negative_price_anomaly) -- explicitly excluded from this bootstrap's own anchor search, not silently smoothed out of the underlying data.
